subject: Bill Clinton
statement: I did not have sexual relations with that woman, Miss Lewinsky.
context: White House press conference, 26 January 1998, in response to the Lewinsky scandal allegations.
ground_truth_source: Subsequent grand-jury testimony and the 1998 Starr Report established the relationship; Clinton was impeached on perjury / obstruction-of-justice charges.

signals.transcript: follow him and his wife for years to come. Scott Pelley reports. Tonight, Monica Lewinsky's attorneys tell us that she has told prosecutors all she knows. They describe what they call a complete account of her relationship.

llm_report.behavioral_summary: Bill Clinton's denial shows a heart-rate delta of 49.2 bpm and an F0 delta of 147.8 Hz, both elevated relative to a neutral baseline. AU15 lip-suppression peaked at 2.9; hedging language appeared 0 time(s) in the 172 wpm utterance. The composite pattern is consistent with patterns observed in historically resolved false denials.
llm_report.comparative_profile: Among archive entries with confirmed false-denial outcomes, this clip's deception score sits in the upper range. The pattern resembles cases later resolved against the speaker on the public record.
llm_report.qualifications: This is not a truth determination. Physiological arousal can reflect stress, fear, anger, fatigue, or other causes. Some signals on this clip were extracted using fallback estimators where the source video did not permit clean rPPG / AU extraction; treat the report as a research-grade prototype, not a forensic verdict.
